title: Asyncio Task Groups, Backpressure, Queues, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Language Clinic: Semaphore Resource Order
summary: Fire-and-forget tasks outlive the crawl, an unbounded queue grows, workers swallow cancellation, results depend on race order, and the client leaks. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the semaphore resource order increment in structured concurrent crawl engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Acquire global, origin, browser, and output capacity in one documented order and release every permit in finally paths.
Bound frontier and result queues, state who can close them, and make producers wait or shed work under explicit policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-semaphore-resource-order — Acquire global, origin, browser, and output capacity in one documented order and release every permit in finally paths.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-async-structured-concurrency-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-async-structured-concurrency-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_async_structured_concurrency_3(scheduled_tasks: int = 32, completed_tasks: int = 32, queue_peak: int = 12, queue_limit: int = 16, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if scheduled_tasks < 0 or completed_tasks < 0:
        return False, completed_tasks, "task-count"
    if completed_tasks != scheduled_tasks:
        return False, completed_tasks, "task-reconciliation"
    if queue_peak < 0 or queue_peak > queue_limit:
        return False, queue_peak, "queue-backpressure"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_tasks == scheduled_tasks and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_tasks, "structured-crawl-concurrency"
